topic: Create and use shared access signature (SAS) tokens
key_facts: For clients using a REST version prior to 2012-02-12, the maximum duration for a SAS that does not reference a stored access policy is 1 hour.

When to use a shared access signature

 Use a SAS to give secure access to resources in your storage account to any client who does not otherwise have permissions to those resources.

Grant limited access to Azure Storage resources using shared access signatures (SAS)

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Feedback 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Summarize this article for me
 
 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 
 A shared access signature (SAS) provides secure delegated access to resources in your storage account.
A service SAS delegates access to a resource in only one of the Azure Storage services: Blob Storage (including Data Lake Storage and dfs endpoints), Queue Storage, Table Storage, or Azure Files.
Stored access policies are not supported for the user delegation SAS or the account SAS.
With a SAS, you have granular control over how a client can access your data.

Types of shared access signatures

 Azure Storage supports three types of shared access signatures:

 
 User delegation SAS 

 Service SAS 

 Account SAS 

 
 
 Important

 For scenarios where shared access signatures are used, Microsoft recommends using a user delegation SAS.
